title: Replace test image from personal namespace to something official
body:
https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/a918c916787fd60a774c42ee6d97cbb73c3e7151/test/e2e/config_arm64.go#L10

I accidentally deleted this image repo a few days ago and recreated it just now. We shouldn't be using personal namespace repos. Switch to something under `quay.io/libpod` . 

Comment from @Luap99 : 
```
 yes that should be fixed in general. But replacing any image might break CI, i.e. some task might expect single image not a manifest list...
The safest way would likely be to create a new tag with a manifest list instead and then update the tag in a PR and see what breaks
```

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@lsm5 Do you have time to work on this? We should get this done sooner than later because with each release we get one more branch depending on your image.

I guess I can take it unless we have volunteers. I guess I would need access to the libpod org on quay ? Or do you wanna create some test repo on libpod and give me access with a robot account or something ?

I have access so I could add you I guess but best to run it by @baude, not sure if have any concerns with how has access there.

I think this could also be something Tim could start working on as it should be not to complex

> I think this could also be something Tim could start working on as it should be not to complex

That'd be best.
somehow i missed this. this looks like an easy fix. i dont think there is `alpine_nginx:latest` under libpod. can someone grant me write access so i can copy and push the image over ??

Well the thing is there is ton of CI test using this image, any change to replace that can break all of CI so these images must be kept as is.
The only reasonable thing is to create a new manifest with a date based tag like the main testimage we use. The repo has no instructions how this image was build https://quay.io/repository/libpod/alpine_nginx?tab=info

but podman history shows the commands so I guess we can mirror that
```
$ podman history  --no-trunc quay.io/libpod/alpine_nginx:latest 
ID                                                                CREATED      CREATED BY                                                                                                           SIZE        COMMENT
ecea49d99daa5bd62ebaef1338f6bc4c948bf2651b139160404f9c1c48fcd85c  3 years ago  /bin/sh -c #(nop)  HEALTHCHECK &{["CMD-SHELL" "curl -f http://localhost/ || exit 1"] "1m0s" "3s" "0s" '\x00'}        0B          
<missing>                                                         3 years ago  /bin/sh -c #(nop)  CMD ["nginx" "-g" "daemon off;"]                                                                  0B          
<missing>                                                         3 years ago  /bin/sh -c #(nop) COPY file:d1a23dac5297c7ec5bfbed40f6673bc203667a1c5b4d8dd5c07ea66ca987b398 in /etc/nginx/http.d/   3.58kB      
<missing>                                                         3 years ago  /bin/sh -c #(nop)  EXPOSE 80                                                                                         0B          
<missing>                                                         3 years ago  /bin/sh -c apk update && apk add nginx && mkdir -p /run/nginx && apk add curl shadow bind-tools tcpdump              17.3MB      
<missing>                                                         3 years ago  /bin/sh -c #(nop)  CMD ["/bin/sh"]                                                                                   0B          
<missing>                                                         3 years ago  /bin/sh -c #(nop) ADD file:5d673d25da3a14ce1f6cf66e4c7fd4f4b85a3759a9d93efb3fd9ff852b5b56e4 in /                     5.86MB
``` 

Alternatively I guess we could mirror the official nginx image from docker.io and see if that works for the tests.

In general I would say try to build the new manifest and push that under your own namespace for now then make a test CI run by adding that to `test/NEW-IMAGES` and see how it works, once it all works I can add it to quay.io/libpod or we give you access to do so.
